topic.what: evolving monotone Boolean functions with high nonlinearity using evolutionary algorithms
topic.how: three encodings (TT, TTw, GP) with penalty-based fitness combining monotonicity enforcement and nonlinearity optimization
topic.why_matters: monotone Boolean functions have structural constraints limiting nonlinearity; first systematic EC study enables exploration beyond classical threshold constructions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hamming distance to nearest affine function
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 best result for n=14 imbalanced GP fit2
characteristics.context: n=14 variables, GP encoding significantly outperforms majority baseline

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hamming distance to nearest affine function
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 best result for n=14 imbalanced GP fit1

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hamming distance to nearest affine function
characteristics.evidence: Table 2 best result for n=8 imbalanced TT fit3 and GP fit1
characteristics.context: n=8 variables, both TT and GP beat majority

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hamming distance to nearest affine function
characteristics.context: n=5 variables, all encodings match or beat majority

characteristics.what_it_measures: proportion of runs finding monotone functions
characteristics.evidence: paper text: TT does not succeed in finding single monotone function for n=14
characteristics.context: TT encoding fails for large dimensions while GP succeeds

characteristics.what_it_measures: relative performance of TT vs GP
characteristics.value: TT better for n<13, GP better for n≥13
characteristics.vs_baseline: TT for small n, GP for large n
characteristics.context: representation bias becomes important as dimension grows

characteristics.what_it_measures: nonlinearity achieved with balanced TTw encoding
characteristics.context: TTw encoding severely underperforms, fails for n≥9

characteristics.what_it_measures: distance from achieved nonlinearity to known upper bound
characteristics.context: n=14 imbalanced gap of 1263; bound may not be tight

good_for: exploring monotone function space beyond threshold constructions
not_for: very large dimensions n>14 without representation improvements
balanced monotone functions at high dimensions
requires: ECF framework or similar GP implementation

novelty: first systematic EC study for monotone Boolean function design with high nonlinearity
three encoding comparison (TT, TTw, GP) with dedicated operators
non-monotonicity penalty variants (fit1/fit2/fit3) addressing Hamming weight bias
empirical evidence majority functions not representative of best achievable nonlinearity
